activities_projects.title: Stomatal Conductance and Environmental Stress
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the effect of pollutants on gas exchange efficiency
steps: Apply a thin layer of Vaseline to all leaf surfaces of Plant A (simulating heavy pollution)
Leave Plant B as a control
Attach dry Cobalt Chloride paper to the underside of leaves on both plants using tape
Measure the time taken for the blue paper to turn pink in both scenarios
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantifies the inhibition of transpiration and respiration due to stomatal blockage
activities_projects.evaluation: Data-driven report on how surface pollutants impact plant metabolism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chemical Mapping of Photosynthetic Products
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the translocation of carbohydrates in the stem
steps: Carefully remove a ring of bark (phloem) from a stem, leaving the xylem intact (Girdling experiment)
Leave the plant in sunlight for 48 hours
Observe the swelling above the ringed area
Extract sap from above and below the ring and test for the presence of sugars
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Proves that food is transported downward through the phloem tissue
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate interpretation of the 'swelling' phenomenon and sugar test results (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Action Spectrum of Photosynthesis
activities_projects.objective: Determine which wavelengths of light are most effective for food production
materials: Red, Green, and Blue cellophane sheets
steps: De-starch a plant by keeping it in the dark for 48 hours
Cover different sections of a single leaf with Red, Green, and Blue filters
Expose the plant to sunlight for 8 hours
Perform the iodine test and observe the intensity of the blue-black color under each filter
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that plants absorb specific light spectra (Red/Blue) while reflecting others (Green)
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative analysis of starch density across the light spectrum (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Measurement of Transpiration Pull Force
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate the suction force created by evaporation
steps: Insert a leafy twig into a tube filled with water
Place the lower end of the tube into a container of mercury/heavy oil
Seal all joints with wax to make them airtight
Measure the height to which the liquid is pulled up as the leaves transpire
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the physical 'pull' generated by the transpiration stream (Ascent of Sap)
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical drawing of the setup and measurement of the liquid rise (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anaerobic vs. Aerobic Metabolism in Seeds
activities_projects.objective: Compare energy release in different respiratory conditions
steps: Place living germinating seeds in Flask A and boiled seeds in Flask B
Insert thermometers and seal the flasks with cotton plugs
Record the temperature every 2 hours for 24 hours
Explain the temperature difference in terms of metabolic heat release
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the process of respiration to the release of energy as heat
activities_projects.evaluation: Graphical representation of temperature changes over time (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hydroponics: Limiting Factor Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the effect of specific mineral deficiencies on plant growth
steps: Jar 1: Distilled water + all nutrients. Jar 2: No Nitrogen. Jar 3: No Phosphorus
Grow seedlings for 2 weeks using the hydroponic method
Observe and document symptoms (yellowing leaves, stunted roots, etc.)
Analyze how specific minerals facilitate different life processes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the necessity of micronutrients for plant health and development
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison of growth charts and deficiency symptom identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Phototropism and Auxin Distribution
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how plants move in response to light stimuli
materials: Shoe box with a hole on one side
steps: Create a maze inside the box using cardboard partitions
Place the plant at the bottom and the light hole at the top
Observe the plant's growth path over 10 days
Research how the hormone Auxin causes the stem to bend
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the role of hormones in coordinating plant responses to light
activities_projects.evaluation: Documentation of the 'maze' traversal and hormone logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Carbon Sink Potential Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Estimate the biomass and CO2 absorption of a local tree
steps: Measure the circumference of a tree at chest height
Calculate the approximate volume of the tree
Use standard tables to estimate the carbon stored in the wood
Calculate how much CO2 the tree has removed from the atmosphere over its lifetime
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies biological principles to environmental mathematics and climate change
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and environmental impact assessment (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Bohr Effect in Plant Respiration (Theoretical)
activities_projects.objective: Examine how pH levels affect gas solubility in plant tissues
steps: Investigate how increased CO2 levels in plant cells lower the pH
Analyze how this acidity influences the release of Oxygen from the cell's aqueous environment
Draft a technical paper on 'Homeostasis in Plant Cellular Respiration'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Links chemical equilibrium to biological gas exchange mechanisms
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of scientific synthesis and clarity of the technical paper (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Evolution of Photosynthetic Pathways
activities_projects.objective: Compare C3 and C4 plant adaptations to drought
steps: Examine cross-sections of both leaves under a microscope
Identify the 'Kranz Anatomy' in the Maize leaf
Research how C4 plants minimize photorespiration in hot climates
Compare the water-use efficiency of both plant types
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands evolutionary adaptations for maximizing life processes in extreme conditions
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of anatomical differences and ecological reasoning (10 marks)
